PLAINTIFF JOSEPH FOXS MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 62294980) FILED BY PLAINTIFF FOX, JOSEPH

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ANTONY COURTNEY, ANTHONY RATTONETTI, PURIN PHANICHPHANT, AND 293-295-297 CHURCH STREET AND 1992 15TH STREET HOMEOWNER ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE HOA WITHDREW ITS MOTION IN THE REPLY MEMORANDUM. MR. COURTNEY, MR. RATTONETTI, AND MR. PHANICHPHANT ARE NOT ENTITLED TO SUMMARY JUDGMENT IN THEIR FAVOR BASED ON THEIR CIVIL CODE 580 AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE. SECTION 580 DOES NOT PRECLUDE LIABILITY AGAINST THE INDIVIDUAL MOVING PARTIES BASED ON EVIDENCE THAT, WHEN THEY WERE OFFICERS OF THE HOA, THEY FAILED TO CONDUCT AN INSPECTION OF THE BALCONY AND GUARDRAIL, WHICH ALL PARTIES ACKNOWLEDGE WAS PART OF THE COMMON AREA, WHICH LED TO PLAINTIFF JOSEPH FOX'S INJURIES. THE HOA HAD A DUTY TO REPAIR THAT COMMON AREA PER CIVIL CODE 4775(A)(3) AND THUS A DUTY TO INSPECT THAT AREA PER MCNALLY V. WARD (1961) 192 CAL.APP.2D 871, 875. PER FRANCES T. V. VILLAGE GREEN OWNERS ASSN. (1986) 42 CAL.3D 490, 507-508, AN OFFICER OF A HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION CAN BE LIABLE FOR HIS PARTICIPATION IN THE ASSOCIATION'S FAILURE TO PERFORM A DUTY IMPOSED ON THE ASSOCIATION. THE INDIVIDUAL MOVING PARTIES' LIABILITY FOR FAILURE TO INSPECT THE COMMON AREA WHICH LED TO MR. FOX'S INJURIES DOES NOT ARISE SOLELY BY REASON OF THE INDIVIDUAL MOVING DEFENDANTS' OWNERSHIP INTEREST IN THE COMMON AREA, BUT ALSO FROM THEIR ALLEGED NEGLIGENT FAILURE TO TAKE ACTION AS OFFICERS OF THE HOA. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-13-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Sean Worsey of the Worsey Law Firm, Tel. No. 1-415-994-1680 appearing
for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Michael P, Clark of the Law Offices of Mcnamara, Net & Ambacher LLP,
Tel. No. 1-925-5330, appearing for the Defendants;
Atty. Eunji C. Choo of the Low Ball & Lynch, Tel. No. 1-415-981-6630 x 269
appearing for the Defendant Norma Torres;
Atty. Gregory S. Mason of the McCormich Barstow & Carruth LLP, Tel. No.
1-559-433-1300 appearing for the Defendant Norma Torresby telephone appearance.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants Antony Courtney, Anthony Rattonetti, Purin Phanichphant, and 293-295-297 Church Street and 1992 15th Street Homeowner Association's motion for summary judgment is denied. The HOA withdrew its motion in the reply memorandum. Mr. Courtney, Mr. Rattonetti, and Mr. Phanichphant are not entitled to summary judgment in their favor based on their Civil Code 580 affirmative defense. Section 580 does not preclude liability against the individual moving parties based on evidence that, when they were officers of the HOA, they failed to conduct an inspection of the balcony and guardrail, which all parties acknowledge was part of the common area, which led to plaintiff Joseph Fox's injuries. The HOA had a duty to repair that common area per Civil Code 4775(a)(3) and thus a duty to inspect that area per McNally v. Ward (1961) 192 Cal.App.2d 871, 875. Per Frances T. v. Village Green Owners Assn. (1986) 42 Cal.3d 490, 507-508, an officer of a homeowners' association can be liable for his participation in the association's failure to perform a duty imposed on the association. The individual moving parties' liability for failure to inspect the common area which led to Mr. Fox's injuries does not arise solely by reason of the individual moving defendants' ownership interest in the common area, but also from their alleged negligent failure to take action as officers of the HOA. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk
